title: Add Next.js middleware.ts for route protection
description: Create a Next.js middleware.ts at project root that uses the existing createAuthMiddleware to protect API routes via JWT validation. The middleware should: extract JWT from Authorization Bearer header, validate signature/expiration, reject unauthorized requests with 401, attach user info to request headers for downstream use, and support configurable protected route patterns with explicit exclusions for public routes like /api/health.
existing_patterns: src/middleware/auth-middleware.ts - createAuthMiddleware factory that validates JWT tokens and extracts user/session context
src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JwtService.verify() for token signature and expiration validation
src/auth/withAuth.ts - existing HOC pattern for per-route authentication; middleware should complement (not replace) this pattern
questions: Should all API routes be protected by default with specific exclusions, or should protected routes be opt-in?
Which routes should be explicitly excluded from protection (e.g., /api/health, /api/csrf-token)?
Should this middleware work alongside the existing withAuth HOC (which handles role-based access), or should the HOC be deprecated in favor of middleware?
